app.title: GentleMend — Breast Cancer Side Effect Assessment
app.description: Rule-engine based breast cancer treatment side effect risk assessment system
app.footer: GentleMend — For reference only, not a medical diagnosis. Seek immediate medical attention in emergencies.
app.disclaimer: Assessment results are for reference only and cannot replace professional medical diagnosis

input.title: Side Effect Self-Assessment
input.subtitle: Describe your current symptoms and discomfort. The system will assess your risk level and provide recommendations.
input.medicationPlaceholder: List current medications (name, dosage, frequency) and any recent surgical procedures...
input.medicationHint: e.g., Docetaxel 75mg/m² every 3 weeks, lumpectomy last month
input.historyPlaceholder: Relevant medical history: allergies, chronic conditions, family history...
input.historyHint: e.g., Hypertension for 5 years, penicillin allergy, mother had breast cancer
input.textPlaceholder: e.g., I've been feeling nauseous for the past three days, can't eat, feeling weak, slight fever...
input.textHint: Describe in your own words — the system will automatically identify symptom keywords

result.title: Assessment Result
result.contactDesc: Your symptoms are assessed as high risk. Please contact your medical team as soon as possible for professional guidance.
result.contactMedium: If symptoms persist or worsen, consider contacting your medical team

feedback.question: Was this assessment result helpful?
feedback.thanks: Thank you for your feedback. This helps us continuously improve assessment accuracy.

risk.msgLow: Your symptoms are mild. Please continue to monitor and record any changes.
risk.msgMedium: Please closely monitor your symptoms. Contact your medical team if necessary.
risk.msgHigh: Please contact your medical team or visit the hospital as soon as possible.

nausea: Occasional mild nausea that passes quickly, barely affects eating or daily routine
Frequent nausea throughout the day, noticeably eating less, sometimes need to rest to feel better
Persistent severe nausea that makes eating nearly impossible, significantly impacts daily life and recovery
vomiting: Occasional vomiting, no more than 1-2 times per day, resolves on its own without affecting meals
Vomiting several times daily, difficulty keeping food down, need to focus on staying hydrated
Severe non-stop vomiting, unable to eat or drink anything, feeling very weak with possible dehydration
diarrhea: Bowel movements slightly more frequent than usual, 1-3 extra times per day, soft but manageable
Frequent diarrhea, 4+ times daily, need to stay near a bathroom, limiting normal activities
Severe watery diarrhea many times a day, with cramping or signs of dehydration, noticeable weakness
anorexia: Appetite slightly reduced compared to normal, still able to eat regular meals, no weight change
Appetite significantly decreased, eating less than half of normal portions, starting to lose weight
Complete loss of appetite, food feels repulsive, noticeable weight loss over a short period
mucositis: Mild discomfort in the mouth, possibly some redness or swelling, doesn't affect eating or speaking
Visible mouth sores causing pain when eating, need to switch to soft or liquid foods to manage
Severe mouth ulceration with intense pain, unable to eat normally, difficulty speaking and swallowing
fever: Slightly elevated temperature (37.5-38°C), feeling generally okay, no chills or significant discomfort
Moderate fever (38-38.5°C), feeling unwell, may have mild chills or headache along with the fever
Persistent high fever (>38.5°C) that won't come down, with noticeable chills, sweating, and feeling very unwell
hemorrhage: Occasional small bleeding spots or mild bruises on skin, slight gum bleeding when brushing teeth
Bleeding that's hard to stop, bruises appearing more often and larger, frequent nosebleeds or gum bleeding
Significant bleeding that's difficult to control, possible signs of internal bleeding like blood in urine or dark stools
rash: Small localized rash or red spots on a limited area, no itching or pain, doesn't affect daily comfort
Rash spreading to multiple body areas with noticeable itching, affecting sleep and daily comfort level
Widespread rash covering large areas, severe itching or pain, possibly with blisters or peeling skin
alopecia: Slightly more hair falling out when washing or brushing, not very noticeable in appearance
Very noticeable hair loss with significantly thinner hair, may want to consider wigs or head coverings
neuropathy: Occasional mild numbness or tingling in fingertips or toes, brief episodes that don't affect activities
Frequent numbness or tingling in hands and feet, affecting fine motor tasks like buttoning clothes or using chopsticks
Severe numbness with significant loss of sensation, affecting walking and ability to perform daily self-care
fatigue: Getting tired more easily than usual, but able to recover energy after adequate rest and sleep
Noticeable fatigue and weakness, everyday activities like housework or walking feel significantly harder
Extreme exhaustion, need to stay in bed most of the time, barely able to perform any daily activities
arthralgia: Mild joint aching or soreness, occasional discomfort during movement but doesn't limit normal activities
Noticeable joint pain that limits range of motion, worse when climbing stairs or bending, need pain management
Severe joint pain even at rest, significantly impairs movement and disrupts sleep, daily activities very difficult
hot_flash: Occasional hot flashes with mild sweating, lasting a few minutes then resolving, minimal daily impact
Frequent hot flash episodes multiple times daily, noticeable sweating, interfering with work and social activities
Severe hot flashes with heavy sweating, night sweats seriously disrupting sleep, frequent episodes throughout the day
cardiotoxicity: Occasional brief palpitations or mild chest tightness that resolves quickly with rest, not concerning
Frequent heart palpitations and chest tightness, symptoms noticeably worse with physical activity, need frequent rest
Persistent cardiac discomfort with shortness of breath or chest pain, even minimal activity causes heart racing and breathlessness
dyspnea: Mild shortness of breath only after vigorous activity like climbing stairs or brisk walking, resolves quickly with rest
Getting short of breath during normal daily activities like walking or light housework, need to stop and rest frequently
Difficulty breathing even while sitting still or lying down, any speaking or minimal movement worsens breathlessness
